Section 1  
I will write a Python function to count the number of occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity.

Section 2  
The function will take two inputs: `text` and `word`. It will convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase to ensure case insensitivity. Then, it will split the `text` into words and count how many times the `word` appears in the list of words.

Section 3  
The function will use the `lower()` method to convert both the `text` and `word` to lowercase, then split the `text` into words using the `split()` method. It will then use the `count()` method to find the number of occurrences of the `word` in the list of words.

Section 4  
The function will return the count as an integer. The code will be written in a way that handles edge cases, such as the word not being present in the text or being present multiple times.

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections.
